dialog: The line is trembling! It's TREMBLING! Lower that slider, come on!
If you take steps that are too big, you'll jump right over the solution without seeing it. Like when you're looking for your house keys and you step over them three times.
Okay, okay! I'll lower it. But what if I go too slow?
Okay... waiting... one more epoch... still waiting... Neura, at this rate we'll finish the day after tomorrow.
A learning rate that's too low isn't wrong, it's just exhausting. Raise it a little, come on. Perfection isn't worth eternity.
So there's a sweet spot? Not too fast, not too slow?
